1→# Omni Lisp — Complete Feature Inventory
     2→
     3→**Last updated:** 2026-02-23
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## 1. Special Forms
     8→
     9→### 1.1 `lambda` — Function Definition
    10→```lisp
    11→(lambda (x) body)
    12→(lambda (x y z) body)     ; multi-param with strict arity
    13→(lambda (x .. rest) body)  ; variadic with rest parameter
    14→(lambda () body)            ; zero-arg lambda
    15→```
    16→- Multi-param lambdas have strict arity: `(lambda (x y) body)` requires exactly 2 arguments
    17→- Use `_` placeholder `(+ 1 _)`, `|>` pipe, or `partial` for partial application
    18→- Variadic lambdas: `(lambda (x .. rest) body)`, rest collects extra args as list
    19→- Zero-arg lambdas: `(lambda () body)` uses sentinel param, `has_param=false`
    20→- Creates closure capturing lexical environment
    21→
    22→### 1.2 `define` — Global Definition
    23→```lisp
    24→(define name value)
    25→(define inc (lambda (x) (+ x 1)))
    26→(define (f x) body)         ; shorthand for (define f (lambda (x) body))
    27→(define (add a b) (+ a b))  ; shorthand with multi-param (strict arity)
    28→(define (thunk) 42)          ; shorthand zero-arg
    29→```
    30→- Binds name in global environment
    31→- Value is evaluated before binding
    32→- Shorthand form `(define (f x ...) body)` desugars to `(define f (lambda (x ...) body))`
    33→
    34→### 1.3 `let` — Local Binding
    35→```lisp
    36→(let (name init) body)
    37→(let (x 1 y 2) (+ x y))  ; multi-binding (desugars to nested lets)
    38→```
    39→- Flat-pair syntax: `(let (x v) body)`, `(let (x 1 y 2) body)`
    40→- Multi-binding let desugars to nested single-binding lets in the parser
    41→- Non-recursive by default
    42→- Example: `(let (x 10) (+ x 1))` => 11
    43→
    44→### 1.3b `named let` — Looping Construct
    45→```lisp
    46→(let name (var init ...) body)
    47→```
    48→- Named let desugars to: `(let ^rec (name (lambda (var ...) body)) (name init ...))`
    49→- Enables iterative loops via tail-recursive named function
    50→- Example: `(let loop (n 5 acc 1) (if (= n 0) acc (loop (- n 1) (* acc n))))` => 120
    51→
    52→### 1.4 `let ^rec` — Recursive Local Binding
    53→```lisp
    54→(let ^rec (fact (lambda (n) (if (= n 0) 1 (* n (fact (- n 1))))))
    55→  (fact 5))
    56→```
    57→- Self-referencing closures via `^rec` metadata
    58→- Creates placeholder, extends environment, then patches closure's captured env
    59→- Enables local recursive functions without polluting global scope
    60→
    61→### 1.5 `if` — Conditional
    62→```lisp
    63→(if test then-expr else-expr)
    64→```
    65→- All three branches required
    66→- Short-circuit: only chosen branch is evaluated
    67→- Truthiness: only `nil` and `false` are falsy; everything else is truthy (including `0`, `""`, `'()`)
    68→
    69→### 1.6 `quote` — Prevent Evaluation
    70→```lisp
    71→(quote datum)
    72→'datum          ; shorthand
    73→'(1 2 3)        ; quoted list
    74→'foo            ; quoted symbol
    75→```
    76→
    77→### 1.7 `and` — Short-Circuit And
    78→```lisp
    79→(and left right)
    80→```
    81→- Returns `left` if falsy (without evaluating `right`)
    82→- Otherwise returns `right`
    83→- Binary only (not variadic)
    84→- Examples: `(and true 42)` => 42, `(and nil 42)` => nil
    85→
    86→### 1.8 `or` — Short-Circuit Or
    87→```lisp
    88→(or left right)
    89→```
    90→- Returns `left` if truthy (without evaluating `right`)
    91→- Otherwise returns `right`
    92→- Binary only (not variadic)
    93→- Examples: `(or 42 99)` => 42, `(or nil 99)` => 99
    94→
    95→### 1.9 `begin` — Sequence Expression
    96→```lisp
    97→(begin e1 e2 ... en)
    98→```
    99→- Evaluates all expressions in order, returns the last
   100→- Last expression is in tail position (TCO)
   101→- Up to 64 expressions
   102→
   103→### 1.10 `set!` — Variable Mutation
   104→```lisp
   105→(set! name value)
   106→```
   107→- Mutates an existing binding (local or global)
   108→- Searches local env chain first, then global
   109→- Error if variable is not defined
   110→
   111→### 1.11 `quasiquote` — Template Expressions
   112→```lisp
   113→`(a b ,expr ,@list-expr)
   114→```
   115→- `` ` `` introduces a template (like `quote` but allows unquoting)
   116→- `,expr` evaluates `expr` and splices result into template
   117→- `,@expr` evaluates `expr` (must be list) and splices elements into template
   118→- Supports nesting with depth tracking (Bawden's algorithm)
   119→
   120→### 1.12 `match` — Pattern Matching
   121→```lisp
   122→(match expr
   123→  (pattern1 result1)
   124→  (pattern2 result2)
   125→  ...)
   126→```
   127→- Up to 16 clauses
   128→- Falls through on mismatch, returns nil if no match
   129→- See Section 2 for pattern types
   130→
   131→### 1.13 `reset` — Delimited Continuation Boundary
   132→```lisp
   133→(reset body)
   134→```
   135→- Establishes delimiter for `shift` operations
   136→- Returns body's value normally, or shift handler's value if shifted
   137→
   138→### 1.14 `shift` — Capture Continuation
   139→```lisp
   140→(shift k body)
   141→```
   142→- Captures continuation up to enclosing `reset` and binds to `k`
   143→- `k` is callable: `(k value)` resumes computation with `value`
   144→- One-shot by default in the interpreter
   145→- Example: `(reset (+ 1 (shift k (k 10))))` => 11
   146→
   147→### 1.15 `handle` / `signal` — Algebraic Effect Handlers
   148→```lisp
   149→(handle body
   150→  (effect-tag arg (resolve expr))   ; resuming handler
   151→  (effect-tag arg expr)             ; aborting handler
   152→  ...)
   153→
   154→(signal effect-tag argument)
   155→```
   156→- `signal` signals an effect with a tag and argument
   157→- `handle` installs handlers that catch matching effects
   158→- `(resolve expr)` resumes the continuation with the value of `expr`
   159→- Omitting `resolve` aborts — the handler's result replaces the entire `handle` expression
   160→- Up to 8 clauses per handle expression
   161→- Handler result bypasses intermediate computation back to `handle`
   162→- Example:
   163→  ```lisp
   164→  (handle (+ 1 (signal ask 0))
   165→    (ask x (resolve 10)))
   166→  ;; => 11
   167→  ```
   168→
   169→### 1.16 `defmacro` — Pattern-Based Macros
   170→```lisp
   171→(define [macro] name
   172→  (pattern1 template1)
   173→  (pattern2 template2) ...)
   174→```
   175→- Pattern-based macros with template substitution
   176→- Hygienic binding capture: template literal symbols (not pattern vars, not gensyms, not special forms) are resolved at macro definition time, making them immune to expansion-site shadowing
   177→- Auto-gensym: `name#` in templates generates unique symbols for additional hygiene
   178→- `gensym` function available for manual hygiene
   179→- Up to 8 clauses per macro
   180→
   181→### 1.17 Collections — Arrays, Dicts, and Generic Operations
   182→
   183→```lisp
   184→; Array literal
   185→[1 2 3]                 ; desugars to (array 1 2 3)
   186→(array 1 2 3)           ; explicit constructor
   187→(array '(1 2 3))        ; list → array conversion
   188→
   189→; Dict literal
   190→{'a 1 'b 2}             ; desugars to (dict 'a 1 'b 2)
   191→(dict 'a 1 'b 2)        ; explicit constructor
   192→
   193→; Generic operations
   194→(ref coll key)           ; lookup by key/index (array, dict, cons, string)
   195→(length coll)            ; length (list, array, dict, string)
   196→(push! arr val)          ; append to array
   197→(keys d)                 ; dict keys as list
   198→(values d)               ; dict values as list
   199→(has? d key)             ; dict key existence
   200→(remove! d key)          ; dict key removal
   201→
   202→; Mutation
   203→(array-set! arr idx val) ; set array element
   204→(dict-set! d key val)    ; set dict key-value
   205→(set! pair.car val)      ; cons cell car mutation
   206→(set! pair.cdr val)      ; cons cell cdr mutation
   207→
   208→; Constructor dispatch
   209→(list [1 2 3])           ; array → list conversion
   210→```
   211→- Arrays are mutable dynamic arrays (contiguous memory)
   212→- Dicts are mutable hash maps with open-addressing and linear probing
   213→- Dict keys can be integers, strings, or symbols
   214→- `ref` returns nil for missing keys
   215→
   216→### 1.18 `module` / `import` — Module System
   217→```lisp
   218→(module name (export sym1 sym2 ...) body...)
   219→
   220→;; Qualified access (default)
   221→(import name)              ; binds module as value, access via name.sym
   222→name.sym1                  ; qualified access
   223→
   224→;; Selective import
   225→(import name (sym1 sym2))  ; imports specific symbols unqualified
   226→(import name (sym1 :as alias))  ; rename on import
   227→
   228→;; Full namespace import
   229→(import name :all)         ; imports ALL exports unqualified
   230→
   231→;; File-based import
   232→(import "path/to/file.omni")
   233→
   234→;; Re-export
   235→(export-from name (sym1 sym2))  ; re-export specific symbols
   236→(export-from name :all)         ; re-export everything
   237→```
   238→- Default import is **qualified-only**: `(import mod)` binds module as value, access via `mod.sym`
   239→- Selective import brings specific symbols into scope unqualified
   240→- `:all` opt-in for importing all exports unqualified
   241→- File-based import with caching and circular import detection
   242→- Auto-export: file imports without explicit module form export all defines
   243→- Method extensions are always global (dispatch is cross-cutting)
   244→
   245→---
   246→
   247→## 2. Pattern Types (for `match`)
   248→
   249→| Pattern | Syntax | Description |
   250→|---------|--------|-------------|
   251→| Wildcard | `_` | Matches anything, binds nothing |
   252→| Variable | `x` | Matches anything, binds to `x` |
   253→| Integer literal | `42` | Matches exact integer |
   254→| String literal | `"hello"` | Matches exact string |
   255→| Quoted | `'symbol` | Matches quoted datum |
   256→| Sequence (exact) | `[a b c]` | Matches list of exactly 3, binds elements |
   257→| Head-tail | `[head .. tail]` | Binds first element and rest of list |
   258→| Prefix | `[x y ..]` | Matches first N elements, ignores rest |
   259→| Suffix | `[.. last]` | Skips to last element(s) |
   260→| Constructor | `(Some x)` | Matches union variant, binds fields |
   261→| Nullary ctor | `None` | Matches nullary constructor (auto-detected) |
   262→
   263→Up to 16 elements per sequence pattern.
   264→
   265→---
   266→
   267→## 3. Data Types
   268→
   269→| Type | Tag | Description | Examples |
   270→|------|-----|-------------|---------|
   271→| `nil` | NIL | Empty/false value | `nil`, `()` |
   272→| `int` | INT | 64-bit signed integer | `42`, `-17`, `0` |
   273→| `double` | DOUBLE | 64-bit floating point | `3.14`, `-0.5` |
   274→| `string` | STRING | Immutable string (heap-allocated) | `"hello"`, `"line\nbreak"` |
   275→| `symbol` | SYMBOL | Interned identifier | `foo`, `my-function`, `null?` |
   276→| `cons` | CONS | Pair (car/cdr) | `(cons 1 2)`, `'(1 2 3)` |
   277→| `closure` | CLOSURE | Lambda with captured environment | `(lambda (x) x)` |
   278→| `continuation` | CONTINUATION | Delimited continuation | captured by `shift` |
   279→| `primitive` | PRIMITIVE | Built-in function | `+`, `car` |
   280→| `partial_prim` | PARTIAL_PRIM | Partially applied primitive | `(+ 3)` |
   281→| `error` | ERROR | Error value | `(error "oops")` |
   282→| `dict` | HASHMAP | Mutable hash table | `{'a 1}`, `(dict 'a 1)` |
   283→| `array` | ARRAY | Mutable dynamic array | `[1 2 3]`, `(array 1 2 3)` |
   284→| `ffi_handle` | FFI_HANDLE | Foreign library handle | `(ffi-open "libc.so.6")` |
   285→| `instance` | INSTANCE | User-defined type instance | `(Point 3 4)` |
   286→| `method_table` | METHOD_TABLE | Multiple dispatch table | internal |
   287→
   288→### Truthiness Rules
   289→- **Falsy:** `nil`, `false`
   290→- **Truthy:** Everything else, including `0`, `""`, and `'()`
   291→
   292→---
   293→
   294→## 4. Syntax Extensions
   295→
   296→### 4.1 Dot-Bracket Indexing
   297→```lisp
   298→list.[0]         ; first element of list
   299→arr.[0]          ; first element of array
   300→dict.['key]      ; dict key lookup
   301→str.[2]          ; character code at index 2
   302→matrix.[i].[j]   ; chained indexing
   303→```
   304→- Works on lists, arrays, dicts, and strings
   305→- Index is any expression
   306→- Chainable for nested access
   307→
   308→### 4.2 Path Notation (Field Access)
   309→```lisp
   310→point.x                  ; struct field access
   311→person.address.city      ; chained field access
   312→pair.car                 ; cons cell car access
   313→pair.cdr                 ; cons cell cdr access
   314→```
   315→- Struct instances use field names
   316→- Cons cells support `.car` and `.cdr` as special fields
   317→- Other values fall back to association list (alist) lookup
   318→- Up to 8 segments deep
   319→
   320→### 4.3 Quote Shorthand
   321→```lisp
   322→'x        ; => (quote x)
   323→'(a b c)  ; => (quote (a b c))
   324→```
   325→
   326→### 4.4 Escape Sequences in Strings
   327→```
   328→\n    newline
   329→\t    tab
   330→\\    backslash
   331→\"    double quote
   332→```
   333→
   334→### 4.5 Comments
   335→```lisp
   336→; This is a comment (extends to end of line)
   337→```
   338→
   339→---
   340→
   341→## 5. Primitive Functions
   342→
   343→### 5.1 Arithmetic (5)
   344→| Primitive | Arity | Description |
   345→|-----------|-------|-------------|
   346→| `+` | variadic | Addition; `(+ n)` returns n |
   347→| `-` | 1-2 | Subtraction; `(- n)` negates |
   348→| `*` | 2 | Multiplication |
   349→| `/` | 2 | Integer division (error on div by zero) |
   350→| `%` | 2 | Modulo (error on div by zero) |
   351→
   352→Binary primitives (`+`, `-`, `*`, `/`, `%`, comparisons, `cons`) partially apply when given one argument: `(+ 3)` returns a `PARTIAL_PRIM` that adds 3. This is a built-in mechanism for binary primitives only — user-defined lambdas have strict arity. For general partial application, use `_` placeholder `(+ 1 _)`, `|>` pipe, or `partial`.
   353→
   354→### 5.2 Comparison (5)
   355→| Primitive | Arity | Description |
   356→|-----------|-------|-------------|
   357→| `=` | 2 | Structural equality |
   358→| `<` | 2 | Less than |
   359→| `>` | 2 | Greater than |
   360→| `<=` | 2 | Less or equal |
   361→| `>=` | 2 | Greater or equal |
   362→
   363→Returns `true` or `nil`.
   364→
   365→### 5.3 List Operations (7)
   366→| Primitive | Arity | Description |
   367→|-----------|-------|-------------|
   368→| `cons` | 2 | Construct pair |
   369→| `car` | 1 | First element of pair |
   370→| `cdr` | 1 | Rest of pair |
   371→| `list` | variadic | Create list; `(list [1 2 3])` converts array to list |
   372→| `length` | 1 | Generic: list, array, dict, or string length |
   373→| `null?` | 1 | Test if nil |
   374→| `pair?` | 1 | Test if cons cell |
   375→
   376→### 5.4 Boolean (1)
   377→| Primitive | Arity | Description |
   378→|-----------|-------|-------------|
   379→| `not` | 1 | Logical negation |
   380→
   381→### 5.5 I/O (3 + effect wrappers)
   382→| Primitive | Arity | Description |
   383→|-----------|-------|-------------|
   384→| `print` | 1 | Output without newline (via `io/print` effect) |
   385→| `println` | 1 | Output with newline (via `io/println` effect) |
   386→| `newline` | 0 | Output newline only (via `io/newline` effect) |
   387→| `display` | 1 | Display value (via `io/display` effect) |
   388→
   389→I/O primitives go through algebraic effects. Raw versions (`__raw-print`, `__raw-println`, etc.) bypass effects.
   390→When no handler is installed, a fast path calls raw primitives directly (zero overhead).
   391→
   392→### 5.6 String Operations (10)
   393→| Primitive | Arity | Description |
   394→|-----------|-------|-------------|
   395→| `string-append` | variadic | Concatenate strings |
   396→| `string-join` | 2 | Join list with separator |
   397→| `substring` | 3 | Extract substring (supports negative indices) |
   398→| `string-split` | 2 | Split by single-char delimiter |
   399→| `string-length` | 1 | String length |
   400→| `string->list` | 1 | String to list of single-char strings |
   401→| `list->string` | 1 | List of strings to string |
   402→| `string-upcase` | 1 | Uppercase conversion |
   403→| `string-downcase` | 1 | Lowercase conversion |
   404→| `string-trim` | 1 | Remove leading/trailing whitespace |
   405→
   406→### 5.7 Type Predicates (5)
   407→| Primitive | Arity | Description |
   408→|-----------|-------|-------------|
   409→| `string?` | 1 | Is value a string? |
   410→| `int?` | 1 | Is value an integer? |
   411→| `symbol?` | 1 | Is value a symbol? |
   412→| `closure?` | 1 | Is value a closure? |
   413→| `continuation?` | 1 | Is value a continuation? |
   414→
   415→### 5.8 File I/O (5)
   416→| Primitive | Arity | Description |
   417→|-----------|-------|-------------|
   418→| `read-file` | 1 | Read entire file as string |
   419→| `write-file` | 2 | Write string to file |
   420→| `file-exists?` | 1 | Test if file exists |
   421→| `read-lines` | 1 | Read file as list of lines |
   422→| `load` | 1 | Load and evaluate an Omni file |
   423→
   424→### 5.9 Constants (2)
   425→| Name | Value |
   426→|------|-------|
   427→| `true` | Symbol `true` |
   428→| `false` | Bound to `nil` |
   429→
   430→**Total: 129+ primitives + 2 constants** (includes math library, bitwise ops, sorting, introspection, type system, generic collection ops, and more not listed above)
   431→
   432→### 5.10 FFI (Foreign Function Interface)
   433→| Primitive | Arity | Description |
   434→|-----------|-------|-------------|
   435→| `ffi-open` | 1 | Open shared library via dlopen |
   436→| `ffi-call` | variadic | Call foreign function with type annotations |
   437→| `ffi-close` | 1 | Close shared library handle |
   438→| `ffi-sym` | 2 | Get raw function pointer as integer |
   439→
   440→**FFI type symbols:** `'int`, `'size`, `'string`, `'void`, `'ptr`, `'double`
   441→
   442→```lisp
   443→(define libc (ffi-open "libc.so.6"))
   444→(ffi-call libc "strlen" 'size "hello" 'string)   ;; => 5
   445→(ffi-call libc "abs" 'int -42 'int)              ;; => 42
   446→(ffi-call libc "getpid" 'int)                     ;; => <pid>
   447→(ffi-call libc "atoi" 'int "12345" 'string)       ;; => 12345
   448→(ffi-sym libc "strlen")                            ;; => <pointer as int>
   449→(ffi-close libc)
   450→```
   451→
   452→- Up to 6 C arguments per call
   453→- x86_64 ABI: all args passed as `long` (pointer-sized), cast via type annotations
   454→- No libffi dependency — uses function pointer casting (same approach as OmniLisp)
   455→- Handles allocated in root_region (survive REPL line reclamation)
   456→- Error on bad library or bad symbol returns error value
   457→- Supported in interpreter, JIT (via fallback), and compiler
   458→
   459→### 5.11 Memory
   460→
   461→| Primitive | Arity | Description |
   462→|-----------|-------|-------------|
   463→| `unsafe-free!` | 1 | Free heap backing of array/dict/instance/string. Value becomes an error — accessing it after free raises "use after unsafe-free!". No-op on int/nil/other non-heap types. |
   464→
   465→---
   466→
   467→## 6. Continuation & Effect System (Low-Level)
   468→
   469→### 6.1 Delimited Continuations
   470→- `reset`/`shift` based (not call/cc)
   471→- One-shot by default, multi-shot via `clone_continuation()`
   472→- Stack captured by copying (x86_64 register save/restore)
   473→- Continuations invalidated when their home region dies
   474→- Thread-local prompt stacks
   475→
   476→### 6.2 Effect Handlers
   477→- Installed on prompt frames
   478→- `signal` searches handler stack top-to-bottom
   479→- Handler can resume via `(resolve expr)` or abort by returning a value directly
   480→- Handler result bypasses intermediate computation back to `handle`
   481→
   482→### 6.3 What Omni Does NOT Have
   483→- **No call/cc** — uses delimited continuations instead
   484→- **No garbage collection** — uses region-based memory with per-REPL-line reclamation
   485→- **No continuations across threads** — continuations are thread-local
   486→
   487→### 6.4 Tail-Call Optimization
   488→Omni implements TCO via an eval loop with `continue` for tail positions:
   489→- `if` — both branches are tail positions
   490→- `let` — body is tail position
   491→- `begin` — last expression is tail position
   492→- `and`/`or` — second operand is tail position
   493→- `match` — clause bodies are tail positions
   494→- Function application — tail calls reuse the eval loop
   495→- Macro expansion — re-evaluates expanded form in tail position
   496→
   497→---
   498→
   499→## 7. Compiler Support
   500→
   501→The Omni compiler (`src/lisp/compiler.c3`) translates Lisp AST to C3 source code using a runtime library (`src/lisp/runtime.c3`).
   502→
   503→| Feature | Interpreter | Compiler |
   504→|---------|:-----------:|:--------:|
   505→| lambda | Y | Y |
   506→| define | Y | Y |
   507→| let | Y | Y |
   508→| let ^rec | Y | Y |
   509→| if | Y | Y |
   510→| quote | Y | Y |
   511→| match | Y | Y |
   512→| and/or | Y | Y |
   513→| reset/shift | Y | Y |
   514→| handle/signal | Y | Y |
   515→| quasiquote | Y | Y |
   516→| modules | Y | Y |
   517→| dot-bracket `.[i]` | Y | Y |
   518→| path `a.b.c` | Y | Y |
   519→
   520→---
   521→
   522→## 8. Evaluation Model
   523→
   524→### Application
   525→- `(f a b c)` parsed as `E_CALL` with func + args list
   526→- Primitives receive all args at once
   527→- Multi-param closures receive all args at once (strict arity — arity mismatch is an error)
   528→- Binary primitives are the exception: `(+ 3)` returns a `PARTIAL_PRIM` (not a lambda)
   529→- `_` placeholder creates a lambda at parse time: `(f 1 _)` → `(lambda (__p1) (f 1 __p1))`
   530→- Up to 64 arguments per call
   531→
   532→### Scoping
   533→- Lexical scoping with closure-based environments
   534→- Linked-list environment frames (up to 256 bindings per frame)
   535→- Global environment searched after local
   536→
   537→### Memory
   538→- Region-based allocation for all Lisp objects (values, envs, exprs, patterns, continuations)
   539→- Per-REPL-line temp regions: push child region before eval, pop after (frees temporaries)
   540→- Closure env promotion: `deep_copy_env()` copies captured environments to root region
   541→- Global defines copied to root region to survive temp frame release
   542→- Hash map entries use `mem::malloc` for contiguous array indexing
   543→
   544→---
   545→
   546→## 9. Type System
   547→
   548→### 9.1 Type Definitions
   549→```lisp
   550→(define [type] Point (^Int x) (^Int y))           ;; struct type
   551→(define [abstract] Shape)                           ;; abstract type
   552→(define [type] (Circle Shape) (^Int radius))       ;; subtype of Shape
   553→(define [union] (Option T) None (Some T))          ;; union/ADT
   554→(define [alias] Num Int)                            ;; type alias
   555→```
   556→
   557→### 9.2 Multiple Dispatch
   558→```lisp
   559→(define (describe (^Int n)) "integer")
   560→(define (describe (^String s)) "string")
   561→(define (describe x) "other")           ;; fallback
   562→(describe 42)      ;; => "integer"
   563→(describe "hi")    ;; => "string"
   564→```
   565→
   566→Val dispatch for value-level matching:
   567→```lisp
   568→(define (fib (^(Val 0) n)) 0)
   569→(define (fib (^(Val 1) n)) 1)
   570→(define (fib (^Int n)) (+ (fib (- n 1)) (fib (- n 2))))
   571→(fib 10)  ;; => 55
   572→```
   573→
   574→### 9.3 Type Introspection
   575→| Primitive | Arity | Description |
   576→|-----------|-------|-------------|
   577→| `type-of` | 1 | Returns type name as symbol |
   578→| `is?` | 2 | Check if value is/subtypes given type |
   579→| `instance?` | 1 | Check if value is a type instance |
   580→
   581→### 9.4 Struct Features
   582→- Field access via dot-path: `point.x`
   583→- Nested access: `line.start.x`
   584→- Field mutation: `(set! point.x 99)`
   585→- Positional indexing: `instance.[0]`
   586→- Instance construction: `(Point 3 4)`
   587→
   588→---
   589→
   590→## 10. JIT Compilation
   591→
   592→GNU Lightning-based JIT compiler (`src/lisp/jit.c3`) — **sole execution engine**:
   593→- Compiles expressions to native x86_64 machine code
   594→- JIT-native support for all expression types including effects, modules, quasiquote, dispatch
   595→- TCO via trampoline: `_tail` apply variants set bounce fields, `jit_eval()` loop catches bounces
   596→- 1024-entry `Expr*`→`JitFn` compilation cache with sub-expression caching
   597→- Per-eval temp regions: `run()` wraps each evaluation in a child region
   598→- JIT GC: destroys all states + clears cache when pool > 75% between top-level evaluations
   599→
   600→---
   601→
   602→## 11. Lisp-to-C3 Compiler
   603→
   604→Transpiler (`src/lisp/compiler.c3`) generates C3 source code:
   605→- Runtime library (`src/lisp/runtime.c3`) provides value representation
   606→- TCO via V_THUNK trampoline pattern
   607→- All expression types compile natively (no interpreter delegation for effects/modules/quasiquote)
   608→- Type definitions and dispatch resolution delegate to interpreter
   609→- Supports: lambda, if, let (incl. ^rec), define, match, begin, and/or, call, reset/shift, handle/signal, quasiquote, modules, literals
   610→
   611→### 11b. AOT Compilation
   612→
   613→- `./build/main --build input.lisp -o output` — compiles Lisp to C3 to standalone binary
   614→- Generates 5 files: main.c3, continuation.c3, ghost_index.c3, runtime.c3, generated.c3
   615→- AOT binaries link only libc/libm/libdl (no GNU Lightning, no readline)
   616→- All 8 expression types (reset/shift/handle/signal/quasiquote/defmacro/module/import) compile natively
   617→- Runtime bridge (`runtime_bridge.c3`) provides interpreter-to-runtime conversion
   618→
   619→---
   620→
   621→## 12. Project Tooling
   622→
   623→### 12.1 `--init` — Scaffold a New Project
   624→
   625→```bash
   626→./build/main --init myproject
   627→```
   628→
   629→Creates a project directory with `omni.toml`, `src/main.omni`, `lib/ffi/`, `include/`, and `build/` (containing the generated `project.json` for C3).
   630→
   631→### 12.2 `--bind` — Auto-Generate FFI Bindings
   632→
   633→```bash
   634→./build/main --bind myproject/
   635→```
   636→
   637→Reads `omni.toml`, parses C headers using libclang, and generates typed Omni FFI modules in `lib/ffi/`.
   638→
   639→**omni.toml format:**
   640→
   641→```toml
   642→[project]
   643→name = "myproject"
   644→version = "0.1.0"
   645→
   646→[dependencies.ffi.math]
   647→library = "m"
   648→headers = ["/usr/include/math.h"]
   649→functions = ["sin", "cos", "sqrt"]    # optional filter
   650→```
   651→
   652→**Generated `lib/ffi/math.omni`:**
   653→
   654→```lisp
   655→(module ffi-math (export cos sin sqrt)
   656→  (define _lib (ffi-open "libm.so"))
   657→  (define (sin (^Double arg0))
   658→    (ffi-call _lib "sin" 'double arg0 'double))
   659→  ;; ...
   660→)
   661→```
   662→
   663→**C-to-Omni type mapping:** `int`/`long` → `'int`/`^Int`, `double`/`float` → `'double`/`^Double`, `char*` → `'string`/`^String`, `void*` → `'ptr`/`^Int`.
   664→
   665→**Requires:** libclang (optional runtime dependency, only loaded when `--bind` runs).
   666→
   667→See `docs/PROJECT_TOOLING.md` for the complete reference.
   668→
   669→---
   670→
   671→## 13. Limits
   672→
   673→| Resource | Limit |
   674→|----------|-------|
   675→| Symbol name length | Dynamic (heap-allocated) |
   676→| Total symbols | 8192 |
   677→| Bindings per env frame | 512 |
   678→| Values | Region-allocated (no fixed pool) |
   679→| Environments | Region-allocated (no fixed pool) |
   680→| Expressions | Region-allocated (no fixed pool) |
   681→| Patterns | Region-allocated (no fixed pool) |
   682→| Continuations | Region-allocated (no fixed pool) |
   683→| Match clauses | 128 |
   684→| Effect handler clauses | 64 |
   685→| Handler stack depth | 16 |
   686→| Call arguments | 64 |
   687→| Path segments | 8 |
   688→| Pattern elements | 16 |
   689→| Macros | 64 |
   690→| Modules | 32 |
   691→| Macro clauses | 8 per macro |
   692→| Module exports | 128 per module |
   693→| Begin expressions | 64 |
   694→| Lambda params | 64 |
   695→| String | Dynamic (heap-allocated) |
   696→| Eval depth | 5000 (stack overflow guard) |
   697→| Registered types | 256 |
   698→| Type fields | 16 |
   699→| Method table entries | 64 |
   700→

file.content:
# Omni Lisp — Complete Feature Inventory

**Last updated:** 2026-02-23

---

## 1. Special Forms

### 1.1 `lambda` — Function Definition
```lisp
(lambda (x) body)
(lambda (x y z) body)     ; multi-param with strict arity
(lambda (x .. rest) body)  ; variadic with rest parameter
(lambda () body)            ; zero-arg lambda
```
- Multi-param lambdas have strict arity: `(lambda (x y) body)` requires exactly 2 arguments
- Use `_` placeholder `(+ 1 _)`, `|>` pipe, or `partial` for partial application
- Variadic lambdas: `(lambda (x .. rest) body)`, rest collects extra args as list
- Zero-arg lambdas: `(lambda () body)` uses sentinel param, `has_param=false`
- Creates closure capturing lexical environment

### 1.2 `define` — Global Definition
```lisp
(define name value)
(define inc (lambda (x) (+ x 1)))
(define (f x) body)         ; shorthand for (define f (lambda (x) body))
(define (add a b) (+ a b))  ; shorthand with multi-param (strict arity)
(define (thunk) 42)          ; shorthand zero-arg
```
- Binds name in global environment
- Value is evaluated before binding
- Shorthand form `(define (f x ...) body)` desugars to `(define f (lambda (x ...) body))`

### 1.3 `let` — Local Binding
```lisp
(let (name init) body)
(let (x 1 y 2) (+ x y))  ; multi-binding (desugars to nested lets)
```
- Flat-pair syntax: `(let (x v) body)`, `(let (x 1 y 2) body)`
- Multi-binding let desugars to nested single-binding lets in the parser
- Non-recursive by default
- Example: `(let (x 10) (+ x 1))` => 11

### 1.3b `named let` — Looping Construct
```lisp
(let name (var init ...) body)
```
- Named let desugars to: `(let ^rec (name (lambda (var ...) body)) (name init ...))`
- Enables iterative loops via tail-recursive named function
- Example: `(let loop (n 5 acc 1) (if (= n 0) acc (loop (- n 1) (* acc n))))` => 120

### 1.4 `let ^rec` — Recursive Local Binding
```lisp
(let ^rec (fact (lambda (n) (if (= n 0) 1 (* n (fact (- n 1))))))
  (fact 5))
```
- Self-referencing closures via `^rec` metadata
- Creates placeholder, extends environment, then patches closure's captured env
- Enables local recursive functions without polluting global scope

### 1.5 `if` — Conditional
```lisp
(if test then-expr else-expr)
```
- All three branches required
- Short-circuit: only chosen branch is evaluated
- Truthiness: only `nil` and `false` are falsy; everything else is truthy (including `0`, `""`, `'()`)

### 1.6 `quote` — Prevent Evaluation
```lisp
(quote datum)
'datum          ; shorthand
'(1 2 3)        ; quoted list
'foo            ; quoted symbol
```

### 1.7 `and` — Short-Circuit And
```lisp
(and left right)
```
- Returns `left` if falsy (without evaluating `right`)
- Otherwise returns `right`
- Binary only (not variadic)
- Examples: `(and true 42)` => 42, `(and nil 42)` => nil

### 1.8 `or` — Short-Circuit Or
```lisp
(or left right)
```
- Returns `left` if truthy (without evaluating `right`)
- Otherwise returns `right`
- Binary only (not variadic)
- Examples: `(or 42 99)` => 42, `(or nil 99)` => 99

### 1.9 `begin` — Sequence Expression
```lisp
(begin e1 e2 ... en)
```
- Evaluates all expressions in order, returns the last
- Last expression is in tail position (TCO)
- Up to 64 expressions

### 1.10 `set!` — Variable Mutation
```lisp
(set! name value)
```
- Mutates an existing binding (local or global)
- Searches local env chain first, then global
- Error if variable is not defined

### 1.11 `quasiquote` — Template Expressions
```lisp
`(a b ,expr ,@list-expr)
```
- `` ` `` introduces a template (like `quote` but allows unquoting)
- `,expr` evaluates `expr` and splices result into template
- `,@expr` evaluates `expr` (must be list) and splices elements into template
- Supports nesting with depth tracking (Bawden's algorithm)

### 1.12 `match` — Pattern Matching
```lisp
(match expr
  (pattern1 result1)
  (pattern2 result2)
  ...)
```
- Up to 16 clauses
- Falls through on mismatch, returns nil if no match
- See Section 2 for pattern types

### 1.13 `reset` — Delimited Continuation Boundary
```lisp
(reset body)
```
- Establishes delimiter for `shift` operations
- Returns body's value normally, or shift handler's value if shifted

### 1.14 `shift` — Capture Continuation
```lisp
(shift k body)
```
- Captures continuation up to enclosing `reset` and binds to `k`
- `k` is callable: `(k value)` resumes computation with `value`
- One-shot by default in the interpreter
- Example: `(reset (+ 1 (shift k (k 10))))` => 11

### 1.15 `handle` / `signal` — Algebraic Effect Handlers
```lisp
(handle body
  (effect-tag arg (resolve expr))   ; resuming handler
  (effect-tag arg expr)             ; aborting handler
  ...)

(signal effect-tag argument)
```
- `signal` signals an effect with a tag and argument
- `handle` installs handlers that catch matching effects
- `(resolve expr)` resumes the continuation with the value of `expr`
- Omitting `resolve` aborts — the handler's result replaces the entire `handle` expression
- Up to 8 clauses per handle expression
- Handler result bypasses intermediate computation back to `handle`
- Example:
  ```lisp
  (handle (+ 1 (signal ask 0))
    (ask x (resolve 10)))
  ;; => 11
  ```

### 1.16 `defmacro` — Pattern-Based Macros
```lisp
(define [macro] name
  (pattern1 template1)
  (pattern2 template2) ...)
```
- Pattern-based macros with template substitution
- Hygienic binding capture: template literal symbols (not pattern vars, not gensyms, not special forms) are resolved at macro definition time, making them immune to expansion-site shadowing
- Auto-gensym: `name#` in templates generates unique symbols for additional hygiene
- `gensym` function available for manual hygiene
- Up to 8 clauses per macro

### 1.17 Collections — Arrays, Dicts, and Generic Operations

```lisp
; Array literal
[1 2 3]                 ; desugars to (array 1 2 3)
(array 1 2 3)           ; explicit constructor
(array '(1 2 3))        ; list → array conversion

; Dict literal
{'a 1 'b 2}             ; desugars to (dict 'a 1 'b 2)
(dict 'a 1 'b 2)        ; explicit constructor

; Generic operations
(ref coll key)           ; lookup by key/index (array, dict, cons, string)
(length coll)            ; length (list, array, dict, string)
(push! arr val)          ; append to array
(keys d)                 ; dict keys as list
(values d)               ; dict values as list
(has? d key)             ; dict key existence
(remove! d key)          ; dict key removal

; Mutation
(array-set! arr idx val) ; set array element
(dict-set! d key val)    ; set dict key-value
(set! pair.car val)      ; cons cell car mutation
(set! pair.cdr val)      ; cons cell cdr mutation

; Constructor dispatch
(list [1 2 3])           ; array → list conversion
```
- Arrays are mutable dynamic arrays (contiguous memory)
- Dicts are mutable hash maps with open-addressing and linear probing
- Dict keys can be integers, strings, or symbols
- `ref` returns nil for missing keys

### 1.18 `module` / `import` — Module System
```lisp
(module name (export sym1 sym2 ...) body...)

;; Qualified access (default)
(import name)              ; binds module as value, access via name.sym
name.sym1                  ; qualified access

;; Selective import
(import name (sym1 sym2))  ; imports specific symbols unqualified
(import name (sym1 :as alias))  ; rename on import

;; Full namespace import
(import name :all)         ; imports ALL exports unqualified

;; File-based import
(import "path/to/file.omni")

;; Re-export
(export-from name (sym1 sym2))  ; re-export specific symbols
(export-from name :all)         ; re-export everything
```
- Default import is **qualified-only**: `(import mod)` binds module as value, access via `mod.sym`
- Selective import brings specific symbols into scope unqualified
- `:all` opt-in for importing all exports unqualified
- File-based import with caching and circular import detection
- Auto-export: file imports without explicit module form export all defines
- Method extensions are always global (dispatch is cross-cutting)

---

## 2. Pattern Types (for `match`)

| Pattern | Syntax | Description |
|---------|--------|-------------|
| Wildcard | `_` | Matches anything, binds nothing |
| Variable | `x` | Matches anything, binds to `x` |
| Integer literal | `42` | Matches exact integer |
| String literal | `"hello"` | Matches exact string |
| Quoted | `'symbol` | Matches quoted datum |
| Sequence (exact) | `[a b c]` | Matches list of exactly 3, binds elements |
| Head-tail | `[head .. tail]` | Binds first element and rest of list |
| Prefix | `[x y ..]` | Matches first N elements, ignores rest |
| Suffix | `[.. last]` | Skips to last element(s) |
| Constructor | `(Some x)` | Matches union variant, binds fields |
| Nullary ctor | `None` | Matches nullary constructor (auto-detected) |

Up to 16 elements per sequence pattern.

---

## 3. Data Types

| Type | Tag | Description | Examples |
|------|-----|-------------|---------|
| `nil` | NIL | Empty/false value | `nil`, `()` |
| `int` | INT | 64-bit signed integer | `42`, `-17`, `0` |
| `double` | DOUBLE | 64-bit floating point | `3.14`, `-0.5` |
| `string` | STRING | Immutable string (heap-allocated) | `"hello"`, `"line\nbreak"` |
| `symbol` | SYMBOL | Interned identifier | `foo`, `my-function`, `null?` |
| `cons` | CONS | Pair (car/cdr) | `(cons 1 2)`, `'(1 2 3)` |
| `closure` | CLOSURE | Lambda with captured environment | `(lambda (x) x)` |
| `continuation` | CONTINUATION | Delimited continuation | captured by `shift` |
| `primitive` | PRIMITIVE | Built-in function | `+`, `car` |
| `partial_prim` | PARTIAL_PRIM | Partially applied primitive | `(+ 3)` |
| `error` | ERROR | Error value | `(error "oops")` |
| `dict` | HASHMAP | Mutable hash table | `{'a 1}`, `(dict 'a 1)` |
| `array` | ARRAY | Mutable dynamic array | `[1 2 3]`, `(array 1 2 3)` |
| `ffi_handle` | FFI_HANDLE | Foreign library handle | `(ffi-open "libc.so.6")` |
| `instance` | INSTANCE | User-defined type instance | `(Point 3 4)` |
| `method_table` | METHOD_TABLE | Multiple dispatch table | internal |

### Truthiness Rules
- **Falsy:** `nil`, `false`
- **Truthy:** Everything else, including `0`, `""`, and `'()`

---

## 4. Syntax Extensions

### 4.1 Dot-Bracket Indexing
```lisp
list.[0]         ; first element of list
arr.[0]          ; first element of array
dict.['key]      ; dict key lookup
str.[2]          ; character code at index 2
matrix.[i].[j]   ; chained indexing
```
- Works on lists, arrays, dicts, and strings
- Index is any expression
- Chainable for nested access

### 4.2 Path Notation (Field Access)
```lisp
point.x                  ; struct field access
person.address.city      ; chained field access
pair.car                 ; cons cell car access
pair.cdr                 ; cons cell cdr access
```
- Struct instances use field names
- Cons cells support `.car` and `.cdr` as special fields
- Other values fall back to association list (alist) lookup
- Up to 8 segments deep

### 4.3 Quote Shorthand
```lisp
'x        ; => (quote x)
'(a b c)  ; => (quote (a b c))
```

### 4.4 Escape Sequences in Strings
```
\n    newline
\t    tab
\\    backslash
\"    double quote
```

### 4.5 Comments
```lisp
; This is a comment (extends to end of line)
```

---

## 5. Primitive Functions

### 5.1 Arithmetic (5)
| Primitive | Arity | Description |
|-----------|-------|-------------|
| `+` | variadic | Addition; `(+ n)` returns n |
| `-` | 1-2 | Subtraction; `(- n)` negates |
| `*` | 2 | Multiplication |
| `/` | 2 | Integer division (error on div by zero) |
| `%` | 2 | Modulo (error on div by zero) |

Binary primitives (`+`, `-`, `*`, `/`, `%`, comparisons, `cons`) partially apply when given one argument: `(+ 3)` returns a `PARTIAL_PRIM` that adds 3. This is a built-in mechanism for binary primitives only — user-defined lambdas have strict arity. For general partial application, use `_` placeholder `(+ 1 _)`, `|>` pipe, or `partial`.

### 5.2 Comparison (5)
| Primitive | Arity | Description |
|-----------|-------|-------------|
| `=` | 2 | Structural equality |
| `<` | 2 | Less than |
| `>` | 2 | Greater than |
| `<=` | 2 | Less or equal |
| `>=` | 2 | Greater or equal |

Returns `true` or `nil`.

### 5.3 List Operations (7)
| Primitive | Arity | Description |
|-----------|-------|-------------|
| `cons` | 2 | Construct pair |
| `car` | 1 | First element of pair |
| `cdr` | 1 | Rest of pair |
| `list` | variadic | Create list; `(list [1 2 3])` converts array to list |
| `length` | 1 | Generic: list, array, dict, or string length |
| `null?` | 1 | Test if nil |
| `pair?` | 1 | Test if cons cell |

### 5.4 Boolean (1)
| Primitive | Arity | Description |
|-----------|-------|-------------|
| `not` | 1 | Logical negation |

### 5.5 I/O (3 + effect wrappers)
| Primitive | Arity | Description |
|-----------|-------|-------------|
| `print` | 1 | Output without newline (via `io/print` effect) |
| `println` | 1 | Output with newline (via `io/println` effect) |
| `newline` | 0 | Output newline only (via `io/newline` effect) |
| `display` | 1 | Display value (via `io/display` effect) |

I/O primitives go through algebraic effects. Raw versions (`__raw-print`, `__raw-println`, etc.) bypass effects.
When no handler is installed, a fast path calls raw primitives directly (zero overhead).

### 5.6 String Operations (10)
| Primitive | Arity | Description |
|-----------|-------|-------------|
| `string-append` | variadic | Concatenate strings |
| `string-join` | 2 | Join list with separator |
| `substring` | 3 | Extract substring (supports negative indices) |
| `string-split` | 2 | Split by single-char delimiter |
| `string-length` | 1 | String length |
| `string->list` | 1 | String to list of single-char strings |
| `list->string` | 1 | List of strings to string |
| `string-upcase` | 1 | Uppercase conversion |
| `string-downcase` | 1 | Lowercase conversion |
| `string-trim` | 1 | Remove leading/trailing whitespace |

### 5.7 Type Predicates (5)
| Primitive | Arity | Description |
|-----------|-------|-------------|
| `string?` | 1 | Is value a string? |
| `int?` | 1 | Is value an integer? |
| `symbol?` | 1 | Is value a symbol? |
| `closure?` | 1 | Is value a closure? |
| `continuation?` | 1 | Is value a continuation? |

### 5.8 File I/O (5)
| Primitive | Arity | Description |
|-----------|-------|-------------|
| `read-file` | 1 | Read entire file as string |
| `write-file` | 2 | Write string to file |
| `file-exists?` | 1 | Test if file exists |
| `read-lines` | 1 | Read file as list of lines |
| `load` | 1 | Load and evaluate an Omni file |

### 5.9 Constants (2)
| Name | Value |
|------|-------|
| `true` | Symbol `true` |
| `false` | Bound to `nil` |

**Total: 129+ primitives + 2 constants** (includes math library, bitwise ops, sorting, introspection, type system, generic collection ops, and more not listed above)

### 5.10 FFI (Foreign Function Interface)
| Primitive | Arity | Description |
|-----------|-------|-------------|
| `ffi-open` | 1 | Open shared library via dlopen |
| `ffi-call` | variadic | Call foreign function with type annotations |
| `ffi-close` | 1 | Close shared library handle |
| `ffi-sym` | 2 | Get raw function pointer as integer |

**FFI type symbols:** `'int`, `'size`, `'string`, `'void`, `'ptr`, `'double`

```lisp
(define libc (ffi-open "libc.so.6"))
(ffi-call libc "strlen" 'size "hello" 'string)   ;; => 5
(ffi-call libc "abs" 'int -42 'int)              ;; => 42
(ffi-call libc "getpid" 'int)                     ;; => <pid>
(ffi-call libc "atoi" 'int "12345" 'string)       ;; => 12345
(ffi-sym libc "strlen")                            ;; => <pointer as int>
(ffi-close libc)
```

- Up to 6 C arguments per call
- x86_64 ABI: all args passed as `long` (pointer-sized), cast via type annotations
- No libffi dependency — uses function pointer casting (same approach as OmniLisp)
- Handles allocated in root_region (survive REPL line reclamation)
- Error on bad library or bad symbol returns error value
- Supported in interpreter, JIT (via fallback), and compiler

### 5.11 Memory

| Primitive | Arity | Description |
|-----------|-------|-------------|
| `unsafe-free!` | 1 | Free heap backing of array/dict/instance/string. Value becomes an error — accessing it after free raises "use after unsafe-free!". No-op on int/nil/other non-heap types. |

---

## 6. Continuation & Effect System (Low-Level)

### 6.1 Delimited Continuations
- `reset`/`shift` based (not call/cc)
- One-shot by default, multi-shot via `clone_continuation()`
- Stack captured by copying (x86_64 register save/restore)
- Continuations invalidated when their home region dies
- Thread-local prompt stacks

### 6.2 Effect Handlers
- Installed on prompt frames
- `signal` searches handler stack top-to-bottom
- Handler can resume via `(resolve expr)` or abort by returning a value directly
- Handler result bypasses intermediate computation back to `handle`

### 6.3 What Omni Does NOT Have
- **No call/cc** — uses delimited continuations instead
- **No garbage collection** — uses region-based memory with per-REPL-line reclamation
- **No continuations across threads** — continuations are thread-local

### 6.4 Tail-Call Optimization
Omni implements TCO via an eval loop with `continue` for tail positions:
- `if` — both branches are tail positions
- `let` — body is tail position
- `begin` — last expression is tail position
- `and`/`or` — second operand is tail position
- `match` — clause bodies are tail positions
- Function application — tail calls reuse the eval loop
- Macro expansion — re-evaluates expanded form in tail position

---

## 7. Compiler Support

The Omni compiler (`src/lisp/compiler.c3`) translates Lisp AST to C3 source code using a runtime library (`src/lisp/runtime.c3`).

| Feature | Interpreter | Compiler |
|---------|:-----------:|:--------:|
| lambda | Y | Y |
| define | Y | Y |
| let | Y | Y |
| let ^rec | Y | Y |
| if | Y | Y |
| quote | Y | Y |
| match | Y | Y |
| and/or | Y | Y |
| reset/shift | Y | Y |
| handle/signal | Y | Y |
| quasiquote | Y | Y |
| modules | Y | Y |
| dot-bracket `.[i]` | Y | Y |
| path `a.b.c` | Y | Y |

---

## 8. Evaluation Model

### Application
- `(f a b c)` parsed as `E_CALL` with func + args list
- Primitives receive all args at once
- Multi-param closures receive all args at once (strict arity — arity mismatch is an error)
- Binary primitives are the exception: `(+ 3)` returns a `PARTIAL_PRIM` (not a lambda)
- `_` placeholder creates a lambda at parse time: `(f 1 _)` → `(lambda (__p1) (f 1 __p1))`
- Up to 64 arguments per call

### Scoping
- Lexical scoping with closure-based environments
- Linked-list environment frames (up to 256 bindings per frame)
- Global environment searched after local

### Memory
- Region-based allocation for all Lisp objects (values, envs, exprs, patterns, continuations)
- Per-REPL-line temp regions: push child region before eval, pop after (frees temporaries)
- Closure env promotion: `deep_copy_env()` copies captured environments to root region
- Global defines copied to root region to survive temp frame release
- Hash map entries use `mem::malloc` for contiguous array indexing

---

## 9. Type System

### 9.1 Type Definitions
```lisp
(define [type] Point (^Int x) (^Int y))           ;; struct type
(define [abstract] Shape)                           ;; abstract type
(define [type] (Circle Shape) (^Int radius))       ;; subtype of Shape
(define [union] (Option T) None (Some T))          ;; union/ADT
(define [alias] Num Int)                            ;; type alias
```

### 9.2 Multiple Dispatch
```lisp
(define (describe (^Int n)) "integer")
(define (describe (^String s)) "string")
(define (describe x) "other")           ;; fallback
(describe 42)      ;; => "integer"
(describe "hi")    ;; => "string"
```

Val dispatch for value-level matching:
```lisp
(define (fib (^(Val 0) n)) 0)
(define (fib (^(Val 1) n)) 1)
(define (fib (^Int n)) (+ (fib (- n 1)) (fib (- n 2))))
(fib 10)  ;; => 55
```

### 9.3 Type Introspection
| Primitive | Arity | Description |
|-----------|-------|-------------|
| `type-of` | 1 | Returns type name as symbol |
| `is?` | 2 | Check if value is/subtypes given type |
| `instance?` | 1 | Check if value is a type instance |

### 9.4 Struct Features
- Field access via dot-path: `point.x`
- Nested access: `line.start.x`
- Field mutation: `(set! point.x 99)`
- Positional indexing: `instance.[0]`
- Instance construction: `(Point 3 4)`

---

## 10. JIT Compilation

GNU Lightning-based JIT compiler (`src/lisp/jit.c3`) — **sole execution engine**:
- Compiles expressions to native x86_64 machine code
- JIT-native support for all expression types including effects, modules, quasiquote, dispatch
- TCO via trampoline: `_tail` apply variants set bounce fields, `jit_eval()` loop catches bounces
- 1024-entry `Expr*`→`JitFn` compilation cache with sub-expression caching
- Per-eval temp regions: `run()` wraps each evaluation in a child region
- JIT GC: destroys all states + clears cache when pool > 75% between top-level evaluations

---

## 11. Lisp-to-C3 Compiler

Transpiler (`src/lisp/compiler.c3`) generates C3 source code:
- Runtime library (`src/lisp/runtime.c3`) provides value representation
- TCO via V_THUNK trampoline pattern
- All expression types compile natively (no interpreter delegation for effects/modules/quasiquote)
- Type definitions and dispatch resolution delegate to interpreter
- Supports: lambda, if, let (incl. ^rec), define, match, begin, and/or, call, reset/shift, handle/signal, quasiquote, modules, literals

### 11b. AOT Compilation

- `./build/main --build input.lisp -o output` — compiles Lisp to C3 to standalone binary
- Generates 5 files: main.c3, continuation.c3, ghost_index.c3, runtime.c3, generated.c3
- AOT binaries link only libc/libm/libdl (no GNU Lightning, no readline)
- All 8 expression types (reset/shift/handle/signal/quasiquote/defmacro/module/import) compile natively
- Runtime bridge (`runtime_bridge.c3`) provides interpreter-to-runtime conversion

---

## 12. Project Tooling

### 12.1 `--init` — Scaffold a New Project

```bash
./build/main --init myproject
```

Creates a project directory with `omni.toml`, `src/main.omni`, `lib/ffi/`, `include/`, and `build/` (containing the generated `project.json` for C3).

### 12.2 `--bind` — Auto-Generate FFI Bindings

```bash
./build/main --bind myproject/
```

Reads `omni.toml`, parses C headers using libclang, and generates typed Omni FFI modules in `lib/ffi/`.

**omni.toml format:**

```toml
[project]
name = "myproject"
version = "0.1.0"

[dependencies.ffi.math]
library = "m"
headers = ["/usr/include/math.h"]
functions = ["sin", "cos", "sqrt"]    # optional filter
```

**Generated `lib/ffi/math.omni`:**

```lisp
(module ffi-math (export cos sin sqrt)
  (define _lib (ffi-open "libm.so"))
  (define (sin (^Double arg0))
    (ffi-call _lib "sin" 'double arg0 'double))
  ;; ...
)
```

**C-to-Omni type mapping:** `int`/`long` → `'int`/`^Int`, `double`/`float` → `'double`/`^Double`, `char*` → `'string`/`^String`, `void*` → `'ptr`/`^Int`.

**Requires:** libclang (optional runtime dependency, only loaded when `--bind` runs).

See `docs/PROJECT_TOOLING.md` for the complete reference.

---

## 13. Limits

| Resource | Limit |
|----------|-------|
| Symbol name length | Dynamic (heap-allocated) |
| Total symbols | 8192 |
| Bindings per env frame | 512 |
| Values | Region-allocated (no fixed pool) |
| Environments | Region-allocated (no fixed pool) |
| Expressions | Region-allocated (no fixed pool) |
| Patterns | Region-allocated (no fixed pool) |
| Continuations | Region-allocated (no fixed pool) |
| Match clauses | 128 |
| Effect handler clauses | 64 |
| Handler stack depth | 16 |
| Call arguments | 64 |
| Path segments | 8 |
| Pattern elements | 16 |
| Macros | 64 |
| Modules | 32 |
| Macro clauses | 8 per macro |
| Module exports | 128 per module |
| Begin expressions | 64 |
| Lambda params | 64 |
| String | Dynamic (heap-allocated) |
| Eval depth | 5000 (stack overflow guard) |
| Registered types | 256 |
| Type fields | 16 |
| Method table entries | 64 |
